title: Media Literacy Integration: Combining All SIFT Skills
titleAr: تكامل التثقيف الإعلامي: الجمع بين جميع مهارات SIFT
learningObjective: After this exercise, the user will be able to apply all 4 SIFT moves (Stop, Investigate, Find, Trace) in sequence to evaluate a complex real-world misinformation scenario, demonstrating integrated media literacy as defined by Guess et al. (2020, PNAS).
learningObjectiveAr: بعد هذا التمرين، سيكون المستخدم قادرًا على تطبيق جميع حركات SIFT الأربع بالتسلسل لتقييم سيناريو معلومات مضللة معقد في العالم الحقيقي.

content.scenario:
Guess et al. (2020) in PNAS demonstrated that a single digital literacy training tip — simply telling users to check if a news source is trustworthy — reduced sharing of misinformation by 26.1%. Imagine what combining ALL four SIFT skills can do.

Integration Challenge: A viral WhatsApp forward claims:
'BREAKING: Egyptian Ministry of Education announces all university exams will be replaced with AI-generated assessments starting September 2026. Several professors have already been fired. Source: Anonymous insider. Over 200,000 shares.'

Apply ALL four SIFT moves to evaluate this claim.
content.scenarioAr: أثبت جيس وآخرون (2020) في PNAS أن نصيحة واحدة في التثقيف الرقمي — مجرد إخبار المستخدمين بالتحقق من مصداقية مصدر الأخبار — قللت مشاركة المعلومات المضللة بنسبة 26.1%.

task.instructions: For each SIFT move below, identify the correct action. Select ALL actions that correctly apply the SIFT method to this scenario.
task.instructionsAr: لكل حركة SIFT أدناه، حدد الإجراء الصحيح. اختر جميع الإجراءات التي تطبق بشكل صحيح طريقة SIFT على هذا السيناريو.

items.text: S — STOP: Pause before sharing. Notice the emotional triggers: 'BREAKING', 'fired', urgency framing. Ask: 'Am I about to share this because it's verified or because it's alarming?'
items.explanation: CORRECT S: Pennycook & Rand (2021) showed that simply pausing to consider accuracy reduces sharing of misinformation by 15-20%. The emotional triggers (BREAKING, fear of job loss) are designed to bypass your analytical thinking.

items.text: I — INVESTIGATE: The source is 'Anonymous insider' — no verifiable person or organization. Check: does the Egyptian Ministry of Education have any official announcement? Check their official website and social media.
items.explanation: CORRECT I: An anonymous source with no institutional backing is a major red flag. Real policy changes are announced through official government channels, not WhatsApp forwards.

items.text: F — FIND BETTER COVERAGE: Search Google News for 'Egypt university exams AI 2026'. If Reuters, Al-Ahram, Al-Masry Al-Youm, or BBC Arabic have not reported this, the claim is almost certainly false.
items.explanation: CORRECT F: A major education policy change would be covered by every major news outlet. The absence of coverage from multiple independent outlets is strong evidence the claim is fabricated.

items.text: T — TRACE: The claim mentions 'several professors have already been fired' — but provides no names, universities, or dates. Trace this specific sub-claim: can you find ANY professor who confirms being fired for this reason?
items.explanation: CORRECT T: Specific claims should have specific evidence. If professors were fired, there would be names, union responses, and university statements. The absence of traceable details reveals a fabricated narrative.

feedback.correct: Outstanding! You correctly applied all 4 SIFT moves in sequence. This is the integrated media literacy skill that Guess et al. (2020) showed can reduce misinformation susceptibility by over 26%. By combining S (pause) + I (investigate) + F (find better coverage) + T (trace claims), you have a complete verification toolkit that takes only 30-90 seconds per claim.
feedback.correctAr: ممتاز! لقد طبقت بشكل صحيح جميع حركات SIFT الأربع بالتسلسل. هذه هي مهارة التثقيف الإعلامي المتكاملة التي أظهر جيس وآخرون (2020) أنها يمكن أن تقلل القابلية للمعلومات المضللة بأكثر من 26%.
feedback.incorrect: All four actions correctly apply the SIFT method. Review each one and practice applying them in sequence to any claim you encounter online.
feedback.incorrectAr: جميع الإجراءات الأربعة تطبق بشكل صحيح طريقة SIFT. راجع كل واحد وتدرب على تطبيقها بالتسلسل على أي ادعاء تواجهه عبر الإنترنت.

whatNotToDo: Don't apply SIFT moves out of order — always STOP first to prevent emotional sharing before verification
Don't skip the 'Trace' step — specific claims should always be traced to specific, verifiable evidence
evidence: Guess, A. M., Lerner, M., Lyons, B., Montgomery, J. M., Nyhan, B., Reifler, J., & Sircar, N. (2020). A digital media literacy intervention increases discernment between mainstream and false news in the United States and India. PNAS, 117(27), 15536-15545.
safetyNote: The university exam scenario used in this exercise is entirely fictional and created for educational purposes.
com_b_mechanism: Structural media understanding builds sustained critical evaluation capability
